2606.30642.title: LeVo 2: Stable and Melodious Song Generation via Hierarchical Representation Modeling and Progressive Post-Training
2606.30642.paper_summary: Full-length song generation must preserve coherence and musicality, render detailed vocal and accompaniment acoustics, and follow lyrics and prompts. Existing language model-based systems face a structural trade-off: mixed-token modeling preserves vocal-instrument coordination but obscures track-specific details, whereas dual-track prediction improves acoustics but requires longer sequences and weakens global planning. We present LeVo 2, a hybrid LLM-Diffusion framework for controllable full-length song generation. LeVo 2 formulates this trade-off as hierarchical modeling: LeLM first predicts mixed tokens for semantic planning, then predicts vocal and accompaniment tokens in parallel for track-specific refinement, while a diffusion-based Music Codec reconstructs full-length waveforms. A central contribution of this extended version is an aesthetics-guided training schedule for alignment. During pre-training, an automated music aesthetic evaluation framework assigns musicality-tier conditions to large-scale data, providing musicality priors before preference alignment. Progressive post-training applies SFT, large-scale offline DPO, and closed-loop semi-online DPO to separately improve generation quality, controllability, and musicality. Modular extension then trains the Track-Specific LM for acoustic refinement while preserving the aligned semantic planner. This schedule separates musicality learning, controllability alignment, and acoustic refinement, mitigating optimization conflict and the limitations of static offline preference pairs. Expert listening tests and objective evaluations show that LeVo 2 outperforms open-source baselines across six subjective dimensions, and approaches leading commercial systems on several listening metrics. Ablations validate the effects of the training strategy, aesthetics guidance, scaling, and hierarchical architecture.
2606.30642.paper_summary_zh: 完整歌曲生成必須保持一致性和音樂性，呈現詳細的聲音和伴奏聲學，並遵循歌詞和提示。現有的基於語言模型的系統面臨結構性權衡：混合標記建模保留了聲樂與樂器的協調，但模糊了特定曲目的細節，而雙軌預測則改善了聲學，但需要更長的序列並削弱了全局規劃。我們提出了LeVo 2，一個混合的LLM-Diffusion框架，用於可控的完整歌曲生成。LeVo 2將這一權衡公式化為分層建模：LeLM首先預測混合標記以進行語義規劃，然後並行預測聲樂和伴奏標記以進行特定曲目的細化，同時基於擴散的音樂編解碼器重建完整的波形。這個擴展版本的一個核心貢獻是為對齊提供的美學指導訓練計劃。在預訓練期間，自動化的音樂美學評估框架為大規模數據分配音樂性層級條件，提供音樂性先驗以便在偏好對齊之前使用。漸進式後訓練應用SFT、大規模離線DPO和閉環半在線DPO，分別改善生成質量、可控性和音樂性。模塊擴展然後訓練特定曲目的LM以進行聲學細化，同時保留對齊的語義規劃者。這一計劃分離了音樂性學習、可控性對齊和聲學細化，減輕了優化衝突和靜態離線偏好對的限制。專家聆聽測試和客觀評估顯示，LeVo 2在六個主觀維度上超越了開源基準，並在幾個聆聽指標上接近領先的商業系統。消融實驗驗證了訓練策略、美學指導、擴展和分層架構的效果。
2606.30642.author: Shun Lei et.al.
2606.30642.authors: Shun Lei, Huaicheng Zhang, Dapeng Wu, Yaoxun Xu, Lishi Zuo, Wei Tan, Hangting Chen, Guangzheng Li, Jianwei Yu, Zhiyong Wu, Dong Yu
